title: How retention registers preserve state
description: Retention registers keep a shadow copy of state on an always-on supply so the main supply can be gated off.
body: Use when explaining how a domain keeps state through power-down. Do not assume ordinary registers survive power-down, since only retention cells hold value on the always-on rail. Actions: identify state that must persist, map it to retention cells, and verify the save and restore controls drive correctly. Validation signals include retained state restoring after a power cycle and the shadow supply staying on through power-down. Risk boundary: non-retention state is lost on power-down, so flag any persistent state mapped to ordinary registers as a corruption risk.
